opportunity_summary: TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.03, Iceotope chassis-level immersion cooling, ~2MW facility. World's first chassis-level immersion deployment. Climate-positive via district heating waste heat reuse. 14% CapEx savings, 90% rack cooling energy reduction.

source_1_title: EcoDataCenter To Deploy World's First Chassis-Level Immersion Liquid Cooling
source_1_evidence_snippet: PUE of 1.03. 46kW per rack, scalable to 100kW+. 14% CapEx savings, 90% rack cooling energy reduction vs air. 2MW facility reference.

notes: Climate-positive: waste heat powers district heating. Iceotope + Schneider Electric partnership. ISO certified.

opportunity_summary: Fjord-cooled mountain hall. PUE near 1.10 achieved. Offers DLC and immersion for highest densities. 8°C constant fjord water at 100m depth. Closed-loop titanium heat exchangers.

opportunity_summary: Underground mine data center in Nordfjord, Norway. PUE 1.08-1.15 using fjord seawater cooling at 8C from 100m depth. notes: Underground mountain facility 700m inside mountain. Natural EMP protection. Fjord seawater cooling requires no additives. Waste heat program returns heated water to fjord or district use. 100% hydroelectric power. Modular build-out across 6 levels, 75 chambers.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.07 via 100% free-air cooling at 66N latitude (avg 1.3C). 4MW facility on 2.5 hectares, 6 data halls. Acquired by Northern Data AG (2021) rebranded as Ardent. 100% hydroelectric power. GPU/HPC focused. Ardent states liquid cooling planned for future builds. Pod-based 180kW system (18 racks). Up to 25kW/rack.
notes: Originally launched 2015 as 2.3MW. Tripled to ~11MW then focused on 4MW colo. Acquired by Northern Data AG Oct 2021 for ~EUR25M. Rebranded Ardent Data Centers. Located next to Facebook/Meta Lulea campus area. Avg annual temp 1.3C. 80km south of Arctic Circle. Ardent brand states future facilities will use liquid cooling for HPC.

opportunity_summary: Underground ex-military tunnel facility near Pori, Finland. 11MW, 8,500 sqm across 9 tunnel halls. Direct liquid cooling (Dell DLC3000) operational since late 2023, up to 80kW/rack. Natural 8C year-round bedrock cooling. 100% renewable energy + own solar plant. 0.5MW modular units. Supports AI/HPC workloads.
notes: Originally created by Ficolo, acquired by Digital 9 Infrastructure in 2022, then merged into Verne Global brand. Tunnels quarried in 1960s by Finnish Defence Forces. Bedrock maintains 8C year-round. Modular 0.5MW units. Both shared and dedicated spaces available.

opportunity_summary: Largest certified data center in Nordics. 24MW IT capacity across 15,000 sqm (34,000 sqm total). PUE target <1.2. Liquid cooling for AI/GPU loads (dedicated cooling options PDF). Gcore deployed 1MW+ GPU cluster (H100/A100). Waste heat recovery serves ~14,000 apartments via Helen district heating. 4MW expansion completed early 2025. 100% renewable energy.
notes: Opened 2018. Largest certified open data center in Nordics. Heat recovery priority: 1) heat recovery, 2) free cooling, 3) mechanical cooling. Connected to Helen district heating network. Gcore contract signed and deployed within 1 month. Flexible kW-based pricing.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.10-1.15. Liquid cooling ready since 2023: in-row CDUs, in-rack CDUs, direct-to-chip, rear-door, immersion. Over 100kW/rack supported. notes: Part of Stockholm South Campus with Stockholm 2 South. Combined 5,200 sqm computer rooms. 2 building floors. OCP Ready for Hyperscale certified. 100% renewable wind power for 10+ years. Utility provider: Ellevio.

opportunity_summary: 5MW facility in Stockholm Kista using proprietary fluid-based cooling from sister company GoGreenHost. Up to 100kW/rack. Current operational status unconfirmed.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.14 across 36-acre campus. 51MW IT load across 6 modular data centers. Operational since 2011. Synchronous replication to London.
notes: Flagship campus. Operational since 2011. Tier III resilience. 100% renewable energy. HVO (Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil) backup generators. Rainwater harvesting for cooling water. Enterprise and government clients. Synchronous replication to London.

opportunity_summary: PUE below 1.2 via fresh-air evaporative cooling. First London DC to deploy this cooling tech. 12MW total client power, 60,000 sqft. opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2 via ultra-free air cooling. 10MW capacity, 800 racks across 6 data halls. 30,000 sqft. Aqua EcoPro+ free cooling chiller units (3x500kW). opportunity_summary: 4MW facility in Farnborough. PUE 1.25. 1,200 racks across 4 data halls (22,000 sqft). Up to 30kW/rack supported. Free cooling chillers. opportunity_summary: DOES NOT QUALIFY. Underground nuclear bunker DC in Sodermalm, Stockholm. 30m below granite. Free-air and mechanical cooling. opportunity_summary: PUE 1.09 via seawater cooling (first in world for DC use, since 2009). Standard PUE 1.2 with chilled water, 1.09-1.1 with seawater. Saves USD 1M annually. Cooling-free over half the year. Part of Stockholm Data Parks. No IT liquid cooling (DTC/immersion) currently confirmed.
notes: Seawater cooling pioneered here in 2009. Runs cooling-free over half the year. Part of Stockholm Data Parks initiative (EUR 29M facility planned). Note: seawater cooling is facility-level, not IT-level liquid cooling (DTC/immersion). Digital Realty acquired Interxion in 2020.

opportunity_summary: 50MW operational (15,000 sqm / 129,000 sqft). Expanding to 70MW with 2 NEW buildings designed for liquid cooling (Feb 2025 announcement). Indirect free air cooling up to 35-40C. Heat recovery: 1.3MW heat per 1MW IT via heat pumps (25-30C to 90C). District heating connected. 100% renewable energy + own solar. Tier III equivalent. Land acquired from Onvest for expansion.
notes: Opened Q2 2020 as Ficolo 'The Air'. Acquired by Digital 9/Verne Global. Tier III equivalent. Combined energy and cooling cells (CECC) with heat exchange wheel free cooling. Indirect free air cooling works up to 35-40C. Expansion is HIGH VALUE LEAD: new buildings being designed with liquid cooling from scratch.

facility_name: Boden Type Data Center One (BTDC One)
operator_company: Boden Type DC / Boden Business Agency
opportunity_summary: WORLD-RECORD PUE: Category 2 PUE 1.025 (instantaneous <1.01). Pod 1 (OCP CPUs) PUE <1.03, Pod 3 (ASICs) PUE <1.003. 600kW total, 180 sqm whitespace. EU H2020 prototype. Patented EcoCooling evaporative system — no liquid cooling. ASHRAE compliant at -30C with <100W overhead for 140kW IT load. Hive Digital partnership.
notes: EU H2020 research prototype. Consortium: Boden Business Agency (SE), H1 Systems (HU), EcoCooling (UK), Fraunhofer IOSB (DE), RISE SICS North (SE). Hive Digital partnership for blockchain/AI workloads. Advanced algorithmic cooling: IT workload, fan speed, and temperature co-optimized. Located near Hydro66/Ardent in Boden.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2 via award-winning evaporative cooling. 2MW capacity, near full. 150,000 sqft across 3 data floors. ~400 cabinets at 4kW/rack. 2N UPS, N+1 generators. No liquid cooling — potential small-facility retrofit opportunity. 100% uptime record.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2 design target. 55MW compute capacity across 6 modular DCs. Mix of direct air, indirect air, and liquid cooling. SQ17 building: 24 data halls at 0.5MW each (12MW total). P1-P3: 6 data rooms at 760kW each (182 cabinets at 4.2kW). Multiple 33kV feeds. 100% renewable energy. Operational since 2010. Supports AI/high-density workloads.
notes: Operational since 2010. Glass-fronted architecture with landscaped gardens. Supports AI-driven and high-density workloads. Modular construction similar to Cody Park. SQ17 is older legacy building (24 halls). P1-P5 are newer modular builds.

opportunity_summary: PUE <1.2, liquid cooling available alongside air cooling, 16MW IT capacity. Waste heat feeds local district heating network. 100% renewable energy. Expected Q3 2026 launch. EnEfG compliant.
notes: District heating waste heat reuse. 100% renewable electricity. SPIE responsible for technical equipment. EnEfG PUE <1.2 requirement met by design.

opportunity_summary: Design PUE <=1.2, air/liquid/hybrid cooling options, 36-40MW IT capacity. Hyperscale facility for AI and HPC. Expected 2029.
notes: Long-lead pipeline facility. Cooling vendor decisions likely not yet finalized — high-value pre-construction lead for cooling vendors.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.1242 (TUV SUD certified — best result TUV ever recorded). Free air cooling only, no liquid cooling currently. Qualifies on PUE alone. Large facility, not a cooling lead but demonstrates market benchmark.
notes: No liquid cooling — achieves exceptional PUE via climate location in Vogtland and direct free air cooling. Cold aisle containment. TUV SUD certified.

opportunity_summary: Target PUE <1.2, advanced liquid cooling + direct-to-chip for AI/HPC, 204MW IT load across 9 data centers. Waste heat district heating for Wustermark municipality. 100% renewable electricity. EUR 3B investment.
notes: EUR 3B investment. 350,000 sqm campus. Cooling vendor decisions may still be in play for later phases — high-value pipeline lead. EnEfG compliant by design.

opportunity_summary: PUE <1.2 at full load, 30MW connection capacity for AI or standard workloads. Kyoto Cooling technology. 100% renewable energy, N+1 free cooling. No liquid cooling confirmed — qualifies on PUE. 7,000 sqm IT area.
notes: Kyoto Cooling (air-based) achieves PUE <1.2 without liquid cooling. Waste heat utilization concept included. Solar panels, green walls. Flexible for AI or standard workloads.

opportunity_summary: SMALL FACILITY ~1MW. CO2-positive data center powered 98% by offshore wind. Waste heat from servers cultivates rooftop algae farm that absorbs CO2. EN 50600 certified. Bramstedtlund expansion planned at 1MW. No liquid cooling confirmed — qualifies on small capacity.
notes: Unique CO2-positive model with algae. Small edge-scale facility. No liquid/immersion cooling found in sources. Qualifies on <=10MW capacity criterion only.

opportunity_summary: TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.05, Diggers direct liquid cooling (cold box with aluminum cold plates), 6MW connected load (A1 NGDC). 8 cold boxes, 80kW compute, 50C output water suitable for district heating. 98% waste heat utilization rate. 100% renewable energy. 8,000 sqm facility.
notes: Diggers is an Austrian cooling startup. Cold box eliminates need for cold aisle. Aluminum cold plates prevent corrosion. Currently evaluation/trial phase. Exoscale also offers sovereign GPU cloud. A1 NGDC launched 2018, EUR 40M investment.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.19 (Zurich West 2), hybrid air/liquid cooling for high-density computing, 40MW from two substations, 100,000-litre cooling liquid storage tanks. Free cooling using ambient air. 100% renewable energy. Prix Watt d'Or awarded. Tier IV.
notes: Prix Watt d'Or from Swiss Federal Office of Energy. Tier IV level. Independent modules. Waste heat recovery. InfraVia Capital Partners ownership.

facility_name: Aruba IT3 Global Cloud Data Center
opportunity_summary: Italy's largest data center. Liquid cooling activated July 31, 2024 for high-density GPU/processor workloads. Primary cooling via 9C groundwater (Alfa Laval heat exchangers). 200,000 sqm campus. 100% renewable (hydro + solar). ANSI/TIA-942-B Rating 4.
notes: Alfa Laval cooling partnership. Groundwater returned to ground with no chemical alteration. Two more data centers planned at campus. Euronext operates 7 European stock exchanges from this campus.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2 via EUR 15M river cooling system. Underground mine water at 15C year-round through 3km buried pipework. Eliminates electric chillers. Saves 18,400 MWh/year, 795 tonnes CO2. Hot water output feeds Euromediterranee urban heating (500,000 sqm). 30x more efficient than traditional cooling.
notes: Partners: Dalkia Smart Building, EDF Group, Cap Ingelec, Largier Technologies, Schneider Electric, Victaulic. First European deployment of mine water cooling for DCs. Circular economy model.

opportunity_summary: Direct liquid cooling (DLC) + air cooling hybrid, 27MW IT capacity across 12,000 sqm. AI/high-density ready. 100% renewable energy matched. Phases 1-2 live, Phases 3-4 completing 2026. New campus opened Nov 2025.
notes: Near Schiphol Airport. Part of Digital Realty's broader liquid-to-chip cooling initiative announced globally. AMS-IX connectivity hub.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.15 via indirect adiabatic cooling and hot-aisle containment. WUE 0.380 L/kWh. ISO 14001 certified. 100% green electricity. Greenhouse Datacenters (co-located operator) investing in liquid cooling for AI. Tier 3, N+1 redundancy.
notes: Greenhouse Datacenters actively investing in liquid cooling for AI — potential future lead. WorldStream is hosted at Greenhouse facilities.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2, multiple cooling technologies: direct-to-chip (up to 130kW/rack), immersion, rear door cooling. Expanding to 150MW. Security levels 3+ to 4. 140+ kW/rack density supported. AI/HPC focused.
notes: Poland's largest data center campus. Multiple cooling technologies available per customer configuration. Climate conditions in Poland exploited for lowest PUE/WUE.

opportunity_summary: Closed-loop liquid cooling, 14.4MW IT power Phase 1 (43MW at full build-out across 3 buildings). Region's most advanced DC for AI. 19,000 sqm colocation, 50,000+ server capacity. Opened September 18, 2025.
notes: Poland's leading DC operator. First liquid cooling deployment in Warsaw market. 5.5-hectare campus with strategic logistical advantages.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.1, world's first gigawatt-scale seawater cooling, integrated liquid cooling. SIN01: 26MW IT (expanded from 14MW via liquid cooling). WUE 0. 100% renewable. OCP Ready v2 certified. European Data Center Project of the Year 2025. EUR 8.5B campus investment.
notes: World's first GW-scale seawater cooling. European Data Center Project of Year 2025. SIN02 ready by 2026 (240MW per building). EUR 8.5B total campus investment. OCP Ready v2 certified.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.15 portfolio-wide. ThermalWorks waterless cooling: 70kW/rack (air) up to 200kW/rack (liquid cooling plug-and-play). 180MW critical power (expandable to 300MW). Zero water consumption for cooling. Saves 2.7B liters water/year vs conventional. 100% renewable. OCP Ready.
notes: ThermalWorks is Edged Energy's proprietary waterless cooling system. 74% less energy overhead vs global average PUE 1.58. 5 data center buildings planned. Expandable to 300MW long-term.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2 with virtually no water for cooling. Closed-loop chilled water via air-cooled chillers. 52MW IT (32MW + 20MW buildings). EUR 1B investment. On-site 100MVA multi-fuel generation (HVO renewable + gas). 22-acre campus.
notes: No liquid cooling confirmed — achieves PUE 1.2 via closed-loop air-cooled chillers with virtually zero water. On-site generation addresses Irish grid constraints. Qualifies on PUE only.

opportunity_summary: Design PUE 1.2, expanding by 4.5MW (Feb 2025). 14MW total. Heat pump system: 25C hot water returned, cooled to 12C, heats local school, daycare, swimming pool, nursery. AI optimization of PUE planned. 100% renewable.
notes: No liquid cooling yet, but PUE 1.2 and AI PUE optimization signal readiness for high-density. Heat pump model is noteworthy for district heating integration. 11MW additional across NL in 2025 (Almere, Rotterdam, Eindhoven).

opportunity_summary: First certified AI data center with liquid cooling in MENA. 100MW across 20 data halls (5MW each). Closed-loop liquid and chip-to-chip cooling. Uptime Tier III certified. HPE DLC partnership for AI hosting. G42 subsidiary with hyperscale GPU cluster tenants.
notes: Largest facility in Khazna portfolio. Steel structures complete as of early 2026. Part of G42 ecosystem alongside Stargate UAE.

facility_name: Khazna AUH6+AUH7 — Abu Dhabi AI Expansion
opportunity_summary: Two new AI-ready data centers in Abu Dhabi broke ground April 2025. Part of Microsoft+G42 200MW expansion. Khazna deploys DLC and immersion systems across portfolio. HPE DLC partnership active.
notes: Part of broader G42/Microsoft Azure partnership. Khazna has existing Masdar City facility (AUH6, 31.8MW, LEED Gold).

opportunity_summary: Largest AI campus outside US. Phase 1 = 200MW, total campus = 5GW. OpenAI+Oracle operated. NVIDIA partnership implies liquid cooling required at this scale/density. Desert climate demands advanced cooling. Massive opportunity.
notes: Joint venture: G42, OpenAI, Oracle, NVIDIA, SoftBank, Cisco. Serves 2,000-mile radius. Abu Dhabi government-backed.

opportunity_summary: stc subsidiary expanding to 300MW by 2027. AI-optimized data halls with enhanced liquid cooling readiness for GPU-intensive training and inference. $10B investment. Also partnered with Humain for 1GW JV.
notes: SASO 2023 regulation mandates advanced cooling including liquid immersion for all new Saudi DCs. stc is Saudi Arabia's largest telecom.

opportunity_summary: Saudi sovereign AI JV between stc and Humain. 65MW Phase 1 Riyadh (March 2026), 50MW Phase 2 Jeddah (late 2026). AI-ready implies liquid cooling. Humain $100B+ program drives immersion/liquid cooling adoption across KSA.
notes: Humain is Saudi government's $100B+ AI initiative. SASO 2023 mandates liquid immersion cooling for new Saudi DCs.

opportunity_summary: WATCHLIST: 300MW across 6 Saudi facilities. MCIT committed to sustainable cooling. SASO 2023 mandates liquid cooling for new Saudi DCs. Solar PV feasibility study underway. No confirmed liquid cooling yet but regulatory environment requires it.
notes: WATCHLIST — does not currently qualify (no verified cooling metric) but SASO regulation and Saudi AI demand make liquid cooling highly likely. Re-check when facility launches.

opportunity_summary: Saudi-origin DC operator with 100kW/rack capability requiring liquid cooling. SASO mandate applies. Dammam is dominant colocation market in KSA (130MW+). Fast-rising player in Saudi's booming DC market.
notes: Dammam is dominant KSA colocation market (130MW+). SASO 2023 mandates liquid immersion cooling for new facilities.

opportunity_summary: WATCHLIST: UAE's largest independent DC operator. $5B KKR investment. DXB5 pre-leased. GCC expansion planned. No verified cooling details yet. Dubai's extreme heat (45C+) demands advanced cooling.
notes: WATCHLIST — no qualifying metric yet. Needs revisit when DXB5 specs published.

facility_name: Africa Data Centres CPT1 — Cape Town (Gold Synergy CDU)
opportunity_summary: Africa's first rack-level CDU cooling deployment. Gold Synergy self-cooling rack at CPT1. Zero water cooling. 3,000 NVIDIA GPUs deployed June 2025 (first phase of $700M Cassava-NVIDIA partnership). Plans to expand CDU tech across ADC network in 5 African countries.
notes: Water scarcity in Cape Town drove zero-water cooling strategy. 12MW solar energy secured. NVIDIA GPU rollout will expand to Egypt, Kenya, Morocco, Nigeria (12,000 more GPUs over 3-4 years).

opportunity_summary: WATCHLIST: Kenya's first GPU-as-a-Service, NVIDIA NCP architecture. If Gold Synergy CDU tech from CPT1 is replicated here (as announced), this will qualify on cooling. Pre-construction = highest-value cooling sales lead.
notes: WATCHLIST. Gold Synergy CDU deployed at CPT1 — ADC announced plans to expand this technology across network. Kenya facility is pre-construction = prime cooling vendor opportunity.

opportunity_summary: Uganda's first Tier III DC. 1.5MW capacity across 3 phases. Indirect Adiabatic Cooling (first IAC in East Africa). Small facility = MW match. Raxio expanding across 8 African countries with IFC $100M backing.
notes: Qualifies on MW only (1.5MW <= 10MW). IAC is air-based, not liquid. Raxio also has DCs in Ethiopia (3MW), DR Congo (1.5MW), Mozambique, Angola (3MW), Cote d'Ivoire, Tanzania.

opportunity_summary: 3MW facility, 800 racks. Raxio claims unrivalled PUE in Africa (no specific number published). Qualifies on MW only. Ethiopia is emerging DC market with Raxio as anchor.
notes: Qualifies on MW only (3MW <= 10MW). IFC-backed. PUE claim not substantiated with number.

opportunity_summary: Angola's largest DC. 3MW expanding to 7MW. $30M investment. 70% renewable. Tier III. Qualifies on MW. On-site water treatment plant = sophisticated infrastructure operator.
notes: Qualifies on MW (3MW <= 10MW). On-site water treatment plant. Hosts Angola Internet Exchange Point. Raxio's 6th and largest African DC.

opportunity_summary: 1.5MW facility in Kinshasa. Qualifies on MW only. DR Congo's first Tier III data center.
notes: Qualifies on MW only (1.5MW <= 10MW).

opportunity_summary: WATCHLIST: Equinix's first greenfield in West Africa. 610 sqm likely = small facility. Advanced cooling mentioned but not specified. $100M Africa pipeline = follow-up opportunities.
notes: WATCHLIST — 610 sqm likely small MW but not confirmed. Advanced cooling mentioned generically. Revisit when specs published.

opportunity_summary: WATCHLIST: Morocco government 500MW renewable DC. Natural cooling systems planned. AI/HPC targeted. Pre-construction = prime timing for cooling vendor engagement. Desert climate = advanced cooling mandatory. Separate from Naver Morocco project.
notes: WATCHLIST. Separate from Naver Morocco 500MW project. Government initiative. Companion AI research institute (Jazari). $1.22B total Moroccan digital modernization budget 2024-2026.

opportunity_summary: LATAM's largest DC campus. Phase 2 operational with liquid cooling up to 100kW/rack. PUE 1.35. 100MW new cluster under construction for 2025. Total campus: 600MW across 17 buildings. Master plan approved. $1.13B Phase 2 investment.
notes: PUE 1.35 is above 1.20 target range, so does NOT match on PUE. Qualifies on cooling (liquid cooling confirmed). Phase 3 under construction = expansion opportunity.

opportunity_summary: 20MW AI-ready facility with liquid cooling CAPABILITY (not yet deployed). Market-leading PUE claim. Renewable energy ready. Expansion potential. Pre-deployment = cooling vendor sales opportunity.
notes: Liquid cooling is 'capability' — facility designed for it but deployment status unclear. This is a SALES OPPORTUNITY: facility is ready to adopt liquid cooling when tenant demand arrives.

opportunity_summary: WATCHLIST: Colombia-UAE sovereign cloud initiative. 3 DCs in Santa Marta. $80M+, 30MW total. AI infrastructure planned. Pre-construction = cooling decision not yet locked. UAE partners likely bring Gulf cooling experience.
notes: WATCHLIST. Does not currently qualify. If individual DCs are <=10MW each, would qualify on MW. Revisit when technical specs published.

opportunity_summary: 7MW facility in Santiago. Qualifies on MW (<=10MW). Oracle FastConnect partner. EdgeConneX is expanding LATAM presence with BOG11 (Bogota) at 28+MW.
notes: Qualifies on MW only (7MW <= 10MW). No cooling specifics known.

opportunity_summary: DAMAC Digital (rebranded June 2025) operates hybrid cooling in Riyadh. PUE improved 1.45 to 1.37 with adiabatic+R1234ze system. Liquid immersion in portfolio. Expanding globally to 4,000MW.
notes: PUE 1.37 is above 1.20 target range. Qualifies on cooling (liquid immersion confirmed in portfolio). DAMAC is also building in Indonesia ($2.3B Cikarang) and Philippines.

opportunity_summary: KDDI containerized immersion cooling DC at MHI Yokohama Hardtech Hub. PUE below 1.07 verified. 50kVA server load, 53.5kVA total. GIGABYTE R282-Z93/R182-Z91 rack servers.
notes: Container-type edge DC; small scale qualifies. 43% power reduction vs air cooling. 94% cooling energy reduction demonstrated.

opportunity_summary: KDDI demonstration achieved PUE 1.05 with immersion cooling at Oyama Network Center. 94% reduction in cooling energy vs conventional air cooling.
notes: Demonstration/pilot site. Exact MW unknown but container-type suggests small scale.

facility_name: KDDI Osaka Sakai AI Data Center
opportunity_summary: Former Sharp LCD plant converted to AI DC in ~6 months. Hybrid cooling (air + DLC). NVIDIA AI infra via HPE partnership. General service applications from April 2026.
notes: Opening early 2026. Acquired April 2025. PUE not yet disclosed but DLC confirmed.

facility_name: IIJ Shiroi Data Center Campus Phase 3
opportunity_summary: Phase 3 building at Shiroi campus: 10MW initial (expandable to 25MW), 1,000 racks, 5,400 sqm. DLC-ready design with reserved pipe routing. Construction began June 2025, operational FY2026.
notes: PUE ~1.2 range. Qualifies on MW (10MW) and liquid cooling readiness. AI workload focus.

opportunity_summary: PFN/IIJ/JAIST AImod: direct liquid-cooled high-density AI servers. Design pPUE 1.1 (free cooling mode), 1.2 annual average. 30 server nodes with 256 MN-Core 2 AI processors. NEDO-funded. Test ops July 2025, full-scale April 2026.
notes: Research collaboration for ultra-high-efficiency AI computing. 7:3 water:air cooling ratio. Supply water temp adjustable 17-45C.

cooling_type: Hybrid cold-plate liquid cooling + magnetic levitation phase-change air
opportunity_summary: 350MW+ campus with 30,000+ racks. PUE 1.13 verified. Hybrid liquid+air cooling system developed with HEC Group. Industry standards development for cold-plate LC in China.
notes: Exceeds 10MW but PUE 1.13 and verified liquid cooling qualify. Originally built 2018, acquired by HEC-led consortium 2025.

cooling_type: Plate liquid cooling (R&D with Shanghai Jiao Tong University)
opportunity_summary: National Green Data Center (3 consecutive years). Best annual PUE 1.15 (2022). ~90,000 sqm. Plate liquid cooling R&D with SJTU. Building 2 PUE 1.27, WUE 0.995 L/kWh (2023). 100% green power for Building 2.
notes: Company-best PUE 1.15 verified. Operational since end of 2021.

opportunity_summary: World's largest server cluster submerged in liquid coolant at time of deployment. PUE 1.09 verified. 70%+ energy reduction vs conventional cooling. Servers submerged in special insulating coolant, no fans/AC needed.
notes: PUE 1.09 qualifies. Part of Alibaba's five 'Super Data Centers'. Immersion cooling pioneer in China.

facility_name: ITCen-Aquatron Carbon Neutral AI Data Center
opportunity_summary: MOU signed for carbon-neutral AI DC in South Korea. Target PUE 1.051. Captures 95% of carbon for green cement. Energy from natural gas, hydrogen, ammonia.
notes: Announced/MOU stage. Exact location TBD. PUE is target, not measured. Coordinates approximate (Seoul default).

opportunity_summary: ICN10 in Seoul Digital Media City with Air-Assisted Liquid Cooling (AALC). Supports 70kW per rack for high-performance GPU servers. KakaoBank AI lab tenant (Feb 2024). AWS Direct Connect access.
notes: Qualifies on liquid cooling (AALC verified). 70kW/rack density. Network-neutral facility.

facility_name: LG Uplus Pyeongchon 2 IDC (Liquid Cooling Demo)
opportunity_summary: LG Uplus liquid cooling demo room at Pyeongchon 2 IDC. Direct-to-chip (cold plate + CDU) for high-density GPU servers. Partners: Vertiv, CoolIT, LG Electronics, GST. Verification started Sept 2025. Largest DC in Seoul metro area.
notes: Demo/verification stage at existing facility. Results will inform Paju AIDC (under construction, 2027 completion).

facility_name: LG Uplus Paju AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (LG Electronics + LG Energy Solution systems)
opportunity_summary: US$441.7M investment in Paju AI DC. Liquid cooling from LG Electronics, power from LG Energy Solution. Under construction, completion 2027. 'One LG' integrated approach.
notes: Major investment. LC confirmed but specific cooling type TBD pending Pyeongchon 2 trials.

facility_name: LG Electronics Pyeongtaek Immersion Cooling Testbed
cooling_type: Liquid immersion cooling (GRC tanks + SK Enmove fluids + LG CDU/FWU/chillers)
opportunity_summary: MOU signed Oct 2025 between LG, SK Enmove, GRC for immersion cooling solutions. Pyeongtaek testbed expanding from liquid cooling PoC to add immersion. LG integrates CDUs/FWUs/chillers, SKEN supplies thermal fluids, GRC provides tank design.
notes: Testbed/PoC facility. SK Enmove was first Korean company to develop immersion cooling fluids (2022). DCIM platform also being developed.

opportunity_summary: AI-ready hyperscale DC at Genting Lane campus. 186,610 sqft, 7 stories. Completed early 2025. Designed for liquid cooling fit-out. Part of S$1.38B divestment to Keppel DC REIT. Lease secured to 2050.
notes: LC not yet deployed but facility designed for it. Qualifies on LC-ready design for AI inference workloads.

cooling_type: Direct-to-chip liquid cooling + rear-door heat exchangers
opportunity_summary: Equinix 4th Osaka IBX. 14.4MW IT power, 4,925 sqm. Chillers and pipes in each data hall supporting DLC and rear-door HX. Part of global 100+ DC liquid cooling rollout.
notes: Qualifies on liquid cooling (verified) and capacity (14.4MW). PUE not disclosed.

opportunity_summary: US$124M investment. 6th HK IBX. Liquid cooling for AI workloads. PUE target 1.2 or lower. 100% renewable energy. Operational Q1 2026. Partners: Dell Technologies, Schneider Electric.
notes: PUE 1.2 is target, not measured. Qualifies on liquid cooling and target PUE.

cooling_type: Direct-to-chip liquid cooling + indirect evaporative cooling (IEC)
opportunity_summary: AirTrunk's first APAC deployment of direct-to-chip liquid cooling. 150MW initial (100MW expansion underway). Design PUE 1.15. 10.3 hectares. Rooftop solar with Pekat Solar. NVIDIA DGX-ready colocation partner.
notes: First APAC liquid cooling deployment for AirTrunk. PUE 1.15 design verified. Qualifies on LC and PUE. 23% energy reduction from DLC.

cooling_type: Hybrid dry-coolers + water-cooled chillers (172 dry-coolers, 86 chillers)
opportunity_summary: Largest single campus in APAC (ex-China). 320MW+ across 9 phases, 8.3 hectares. Design PUE 1.15. 132kV onsite substation. 80% less water than traditional DC.
notes: PUE 1.15 qualifies. Liquid cooling not explicitly confirmed but infrastructure could support it. Water-cooled chillers are part of cooling plant.

facility_name: Bridge Data Centres Ulu Tiram Campus
opportunity_summary: 400MW campus with Malaysia's first large-scale Water Treatment Plant for cooling. Cold plate LC deployed at scale for AI workloads. Among first hyperscale operators in region with LC at scale.
notes: Exceeds 10MW but qualifies on verified liquid cooling. WTP operational since 2025.

opportunity_summary: 12MW capacity. Completed July 2025 (ahead of schedule). Optional liquid cooling system for customers. Brings AIMS total to 100MW+ in Klang Valley. Largest DC capacity in Cyberjaya.
notes: Qualifies on 12MW capacity and liquid cooling option. Completed ahead of Q2 2025 target.

facility_name: Submer Madhya Pradesh Liquid-Cooled AI DC
operator_company: Submer Technologies / Government of Madhya Pradesh
cooling_type: Immersion cooling + direct-to-chip + hybrid liquid cooling
opportunity_summary: MOU signed July 28, 2025 for up to 1GW of liquid-cooled AI DCs in Madhya Pradesh. Zero water consumption design. Immersion + DLC + hybrid methods. Local supply chain development included.
notes: MOU stage, locations TBD. Coordinates approximate (Bhopal). 1GW total target across multiple facilities. Qualifies on liquid cooling commitment.

state_province_region: Australian Capital Territory / New South Wales
opportunity_summary: Proprietary LiquidCore cooling technology across all campuses. WUE 0.01 via closed-loop (fill once, circulate for life). Zero potable water consumption. Saves 5 gigalitres/year. Nearly 20 years of liquid cooling experience.
notes: Pioneer in Australian liquid cooling. Exact PUE not found in sources but WUE 0.01 is industry-leading. Multiple campus locations.
